Framework · free to use

A 90-day AI rollout plan for mid-market operators.

This is the operations-focused companion to the AI implementation roadmap — the week-by-week checklist for the operator (COO, Chief of Staff, Head of Ops) actually running the rollout. Less strategy, more checklist.

By Bill Colbert · Founder, Treetop Growth Strategy
Published May 2026 · More from the library
Who this is for

The operator who has to run it

Your CEO has said yes to AI. Now you have to make it happen. This checklist is the version you can print, tape to your monitor, and check off as you go.

The rollout fails the same way the strategy fails — too many things tried at once, nobody owning execution. If you have to choose between doing all of this and doing the first 30% well, choose the 30%.

Days 1-30

Setup & first win

  • CEO has stated the goal in writing — one paragraph, shared with the company.
  • One named AI lead, 8-12 hrs/week minimum, with calendar protected.
  • Audit of current AI usage complete (paid licenses + shadow IT).
  • Top 10 candidate workflows inventoried and ranked by leverage.
  • Top 3 workflows selected — with named workflow owner per workflow.
  • Enterprise AI platform selected and seats provisioned for affected users.
  • Data classification rules confirmed: what can go into AI tools, what cannot.
  • First workflow shipped — actually used in production, not just demoed.
  • Measured baseline for first workflow recorded (time, output, quality).
Days 31-60

Two more workflows

  • Workflow #2 in production with named owner and weekly review cadence.
  • Workflow #3 in production with named owner and weekly review cadence.
  • Measured impact recorded for workflows #1, #2, and #3 (time saved, cycle time).
  • Weekly office hours with the AI lead established and attended.
  • Internal Slack channel or equivalent for sharing prompts and patterns.
  • First playbook documented: "How we use AI for [workflow #1]" — stored where everyone can find it.
  • Identified at least one workflow that should NOT use AI yet and documented why.
Days 61-90

Scale & govern

  • Adjacent workflows shipped (2-3 expansions of existing workflows).
  • All-team training session held — workflow owners present what they built.
  • One-page AI Usage Policy published and emailed to the team.
  • Q2 priority workflows selected with owners assigned.
  • First 90-day impact review prepared for CEO/exec team: hours saved, cycle time changes, revenue impact (if any), next quarter ask.
  • Vendor relationships established (your CSM at the AI platform has your number).
  • Backlog of next-quarter workflows triaged and prioritized.
Risk register

What to watch for and how to respond

RiskSignalResponse
Adoption stalls at the AI leadOnly the AI lead uses the tools. Workflows have not transferred to owners.Schedule pair-sessions: AI lead works alongside the workflow owner until owner is solo for 3 consecutive uses.
Quality driftCustomer complaints, brand voice slips, factual errors in outputs.Add a human-review checkpoint before any output ships. Audit 10 random outputs weekly.
Data leakageConfidential information appears in shared chats or non-approved tools.Suspend access. Notify owner. Issue refresher on data classifications. Document incident.
Cost overrunPer-seat or per-token costs growing faster than usage value.Move from per-token model to a fixed-seat tier. Audit which seats are actually used.
Team pushbackVisible resistance, "I prefer doing it myself" comments.Investigate. Usually a sign the tool is being forced into a workflow it does not fit. Adjust scope, not pressure.
